sections.concept: Arranging people/objects in specific geometric patterns based on constraints.
sections.solvingTechnique: Determine Left/Right based on facing direction. For Circle(In): Left is Clockwise. For Circle(Out): Left is Anti-CW.

sections.concept: Linear (North/South), Circular, Square Table, Parallel Rows.
sections.solvingTechnique: Fix one person first. Relative positions are easier to place.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Circle (Facing In): A left of B.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Place B.
Step 2: Left is CW. Place A next to B in CW direction.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Row (North): A is 3rd to left of B.
examples.solution:
Step 1: A _ _ B.
Step 2: Standard Left.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Square Table: A, B, C, D at corners facing center. A opp C. B opp D. A is right of B.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Place B.
Step 2: Right of B is A.
Step 3: Opp A is C.
Step 4: Opp B is D.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: 5 friends Row South. A in middle. B right of A.
examples.solution:
Step 1: South facing: Right is our Left.
Step 2: _ _ A _ _.
Step 3: Right of A is slot 2 (from left of page). _ B A _ _.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Circle 6 people. A opp B. C opp D. E opp F.
examples.solution:
Step 1: 3 pairs of opposites.
Step 2: Symmetric.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Linear: A next to B. C next to D. D not next to E. E on left end.
examples.solution:
Step 1: E _ _ _ _.
Step 2: D not next to E -> Slot 2 is NOT D.
Step 3: C next to D.
Step 4: A next to B.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: 7 people row. M at left end. N is neighbour of M.
examples.solution: Step 1: M N _ _ _ _ _.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Circle: P is between Q and R.
examples.solution: Step 1: Q P R or R P Q.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Row: X is 4th to right of Y.
examples.solution: Step 1: Y _ _ _ X.

examples.question: 🧩 Basics: Hexagon Table: 6 people. A, B, C, D, E, F.
examples.solution: Step 1: Same as circle logic.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: 8 people Circle Facing Center. A 4th right of B. C 2nd left of B.
examples.solution:
Step 1: A 4th right of B (in 8 people) means Opposite.
Step 2: B (Bottom). A (Top).
Step 3: C 2nd left B -> Left is CW. B, gap, C.
Step 4: Positions set.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: 5 people facing North. A is right of B. E is left of B but right of C. A is left of D.
examples.solution:
Step 1: B A.
Step 2: C E B.
Step 3: A D.
Step 4: C E B A D.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: 6 people Triangle Table. 3 Corner, 3 Side. A (Corner). B immed right A.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Place A at Corner.
Step 2: Right of A is Side. B is at Side.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: 8 people Parallel Rows (4 each). P,Q,R,S (South). A,B,C,D (North). P faces B.
examples.solution:
Step 1: P (Row 1) ↓. B (Row 2) ↑.
Step 2: Aligned vertically.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: One row 7 people. A is 4th from left. B is left of A.
examples.solution:
Step 1: _ _ _ A _ _ _.
Step 2: B in 1,2, or 3.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: Circle 8 people. A not neighbour of B or C. D is neighbour of A.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Place A. Spots 2, 8 are D and ? (cannot be B/C).
Step 2: Spots 3, 7 cannot be B/C? No constraint on 3/7.
Step 3: Use D to anchor.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: Square table 8 people. Corner (Out), Side (In). A at Corner.
examples.solution:
Step 1: A faces Out. Left is Anti-CW.
Step 2: Be careful with mixed directions.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: 6 people row. A and F at ends. C next to A. D next to F.
examples.solution:
Step 1: A C _ _ D F.
Step 2: Rem B, E in center.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: Circle 7 people. A, B, C... G. G is 2nd left of C. C is neighbour of F.
examples.solution:
Step 1: C, gap, G.
Step 2: F is nbr C -> F could be between C/G or other side.

examples.question: ⚙️ Exam Level: Uncertain row: P 3rd from left. Q 5th from right. 4 between P and Q.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Case 1: P _ _ _ _ Q. (Total 3+4+5=12).
Step 2: Case 2: Overlap is possible? P is 3. Q is 5 from R. 4 between. 
Step 3: Overlap formula L+R-gap-2 => 3+5-4-2 = 2. 
Step 4: Possible.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 12 people, 2 parallel rows. Row 1 (A-F) South. Row 2 (P-U) North. E sits 3rd to right of B. B faces P. S sits 2nd to left of P.
examples.solution:
Step 1: B (Row 1). Right is Left on Page. E _ _ B? No. B _ _ E.
Step 2: B faces P. P is under B.
Step 3: S 2nd left P -> S _ P.
Step 4: Layout: 
 R1: ? ? B ? ? E
 R2: S ? P ? ? ?

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 8 people Circle. Some In, Some Out. A sits 3rd right of B. B faces Out. C sits 2nd left A.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Fix B (Out). Right is ACW. B _ _ A.
Step 2: A's direction unknown. 
Step 3: If A In -> Left is CW. A, gap, C. 
Step 4: If A Out -> Left is ACW. A, gap, C (Overlap with B?).

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 9 people linear row North. A 4th from left. B 2nd right of A. C 3rd left of B. D neighbour of C.
examples.solution:
Step 1: _ _ _ A _ B _ _ _. (A@4, B@6).
Step 2: C 3rd left B -> 6-3=3. C@3.
Step 3: D nbr C -> D@2 or D@4(A)? A is ocupied. D@2.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: Rectangular table. 4 on long side, 2 on short. 12 people.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Draw diagram first.
Step 2: Be aware of 'Opposite' meaning (Diagonal vs Direct).

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 8 friends A-H. A sits 3rd to left of G. Only 2 people between E and F. F sits 2nd to right of A. H is not neighbour of A or E.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Assume Circle In.
Step 2: G _ _ A.
Step 3: A _ F (2nd Right).
Step 4: E/F gap 2. F _ _ E.
Step 5: Fill H based on elimination.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 10 people circular. 5 men 5 women. No two women together. A (Man) between B(W) and C(W).
examples.solution:
Step 1: Alternating pattern M W M W...
Step 2: Fix A. B and C are neighbours.
Step 3: Solve relative to A.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 8 people. A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H. Linear. Some North, Same South. A is North. B is South. Not more than 2 adjacent have same direction.
examples.solution:
Step 1: N, N, S, S, N... pattern constraint.
Step 2: Logic requires fitting constraints.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: Concentric squares. 4 people inner, 4 outer. Inner face out, Outer face in. Facing each other.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Map Inner-Outer pairs.
Step 2: Treat as single 8-person system with alignment.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 7 people row. A, B... G. Just as many people to left of B as to right of G.
examples.solution:
Step 1: B and G are symmetric positions (e.g. 1 and 7, 2 and 6).
Step 2: Reduces possibilities.

examples.question: 🚀 Placement Level: 8 people circle. A is 2nd left of B. B is 3rd right of C. D is 2nd left C. E neighbour of A and D.
examples.solution:
Step 1: C _ _ B (3rd Right).
Step 2: B A _ (2nd Left of B? B is In? Assume In). B _ A.
Step 3: D 2nd left C -> D _ C.
Step 4: E nbr A, D. Check if space exists.

shortcuts: Circle with 8 people: Person opposite is 4 places away.
Row: 'A is 4th to right of B' -> 3 people between A and B.
Symmetry: 'Same number left of A as right of B' -> Positions sum to N+1.
commonMistakes: Mistake: Mixing up Left/Right for South facing rows.
Mistake: In Circle (Out), treating Left as CW (It's ACW).
Mistake: Assuming 'Neighbour' means a specific side.
placementTips: Start with the condition that fixes a person's position definitely.
Draw the empty slots first (e.g., 8 dashes for linear).
Use initials (A, B) instead of full names to save time.
